metadata.summary: Simple Python script to perform magnetic resonance spectroscopy experiments.

documentation:
# QuackSeq

QuackSeq is a tool for programming pulse sequences for magnetic resonance experiments.

The general  idea  is that different QuackSeq sequences can be run on either hardware spectrometers or software simulators.

Different QuackSeq interpreters can be used to run the same sequence on different spectrometers or simulators.

## Installation

Create a new virtual environment and install QuackSeq:

```bash
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install quackseq
```

## Modules

- [quackseq-simulator](https://github.com/nqrduck/quackseq-simulator): A simulator for QuackSeq sequences.
- [quackseq-limenqr](https://github.com/nqrduck/quackseq-limenqr): A QuackSeq interpreter for the LimeNQR spectrometer.

modules.name: quackseq.signalprocessing
documentation: Helper used for signal processing.

classes.name: quackseq.signalprocessing.SignalProcessing

methods.name: quackseq.signalprocessing.SignalProcessing.fft
documentation:
This method calculates the FFT of the time domain data.

Args:
    tdx (np.array): Time domain x data in seconds.
    tdy (np.array): Time domain magnitude y data.
    freq_shift (float): Frequency shift in MHz - this can be useful if the spectrometer has it's frequency data in the IF band.
    zero_padding (float): Zero padding to be used in the FFT.

Returns:
    np.array: Frequency domain x data in MHz.
    np.array: Frequency domain magnitude y data.

methods.name: quackseq.signalprocessing.SignalProcessing.baseline_correction
documentation:
This method calculates the baseline correction of the frequency domain data.

Args:
    fdx (np.array): Frequency domain x data in MHz.
    fdy (np.array): Frequency domain magnitude y data.
    order (int): Order of the polynomial used for baseline correction.

Returns:
    np.array: Frequency domain magnitude y data with baseline correction.

methods.name: quackseq.signalprocessing.SignalProcessing.apodization
documentation:
This method calculates the apodization of the time domain data.

Args:
    tdx (np.array): Time domain x data in seconds.
    tdy (np.array): Time domain magnitude y data.
    apodization_function (sympy.Expr): Apodization function.

Returns:
    np.array: Time domain magnitude y data with apodization.

methods.name: quackseq.signalprocessing.SignalProcessing.peak_picking
documentation:
This method calculates the peak picking of the frequency domain data.

Args:
    fdx (np.array): Frequency domain x data in MHz.
    fdy (np.array): Frequency domain magnitude y data.
    threshold (float): Threshold for peak picking.

Returns:
    list: x,y data of the peaks.
This class provides various signal processing methods that can then be used by nqrduck modules.

modules.name: quackseq.helpers
documentation: Helper used for unit conversion.

classes.name: quackseq.helpers.UnitConverter

methods.name: quackseq.helpers.UnitConverter.to_decimal
documentation:
This method checks if the last character of the string is a suffix.

The available suffixes are:
- n for nano
- u for micro
- m for milli

Args:
    value (str): The value to be converted

Returns:
    decimal.Decimal: The converted value

methods.name: quackseq.helpers.UnitConverter.to_float
documentation:
This method checks if the last character of the string is a suffix.

The available suffixes are:
- n for nano
- u for micro
- m for milli

Args:
    value (str): The value to be converted

Returns:
    float: The converted value
This class provides methods for unit conversion.

modules.name: quackseq
documentation: The base module for the quackseq pulse programming library.

modules.name: quackseq.phase_table
documentation: The phase table module contains the PhaseTable class, which interprets the TX parameters of a QuackSequence object and then generates a table of different phase values and signs for each phasecycle.

classes.name: quackseq.phase_table.PhaseTable

methods.name: quackseq.phase_table.PhaseTable.__init__
documentation: Initializes the phase table.

methods.name: quackseq.phase_table.PhaseTable.generate_phase_array
documentation:
Generate a list of phases for each phasecycle in the sequence.

Returns:
    phase_array (np.array): A table of phase values for each phasecycle.
                            The columns are the values for the different TX pulse parameters and the rows are the different phase cycles.

methods.name: quackseq.phase_table.PhaseTable.phase_array
documentation: The phase array of the sequence.

methods.name: quackseq.phase_table.PhaseTable.n_phase_cycles
documentation: The number of phase cycles in the sequence.

methods.name: quackseq.phase_table.PhaseTable.n_parameters
documentation: The number of TX pulse parameters in the sequence.
A phase table interprets the TX parameters of a QuackSequence object and then generates a table of different phase values and signs for each phasecycle.

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_controller.SpectrometerController.translate_rx_event

This method translates the RX event of the pulse sequence to the limr object.

Returns:
tuple: A tuple containing the start and stop time of the RX event in µs and the phase of the RX event and the phase as a list of different phase values for each phasecycle.

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_controller.SpectrometerController.calculate_simulation_length
documentation:
This method calculates the simulation length based on the settings and the pulse sequence.

Returns:
    float: The simulation length in seconds.
The base class for all spectrometer controllers.

It contains the settings and pulse parameters of the spectrometer.

Add a new event to the pulse sequence.

Args:

Create a new event and return it.

Returns:

documentation:
Deletes an event from the pulse sequence.

Args:

documentation:
Returns an event by name.

Args:

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.to_json
documentation:
Returns a dict with all the data in the pulse sequence.

Returns:
    dict: The dict with the sequence data
Loading and saving of pulse sequences

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.load_sequence
documentation:
Loads a pulse sequence from a dict.

The pulse paramter options are needed to load the parameters
and make sure the correct spectrometer is active.

Args:
    sequence (dict): The dict with the sequence data
    pulse_parameter_options (dict): The dict with the pulse parameter options

Returns:
    PulseSequence: The loaded pulse sequence

Raises:
    KeyError: If the pulse parameter options are not the same as the ones in the pulse sequence

inner_classes.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.Variable

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.Variable.name
documentation: The name of the variable.

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.Variable.values
documentation: The values of the variable. This is a list of values that the variable can take.

A variable is a parameter that can be used within a pulsesequence as a placeholder.

For example the event duration a Variable with name a can be set. This variable can then be set to a list of different values.
On execution of the pulse sequence the event duration will be set to the first value in the list.
Then the pulse sequence will be executed with the second value of the list. This is repeated until the pulse sequence has
been executed with all values in the list.
Automation of pulse sequences

inner_classes.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.VariableGroup

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.VariableGroup.name
documentation: The name of the variable group.

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.PulseSequence.VariableGroup.variables
documentation: The variables in the group. This is a list of variables.

Variables can be grouped together.

If we have groups a and b the pulse sequence will be executed for all combinations of variables in a and b.

A pulse sequence is a collection of events that are executed in a certain order.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the pulse sequence
    version (str): The version of the pulse sequence

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the pulse sequence
    events (list): The events of the pulse sequence
    pulse_parameter_options (dict): The pulse parameter options

classes.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.__init__
documentation: Initializes the pulse sequence.

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.update_options
documentation: Updates the options of the pulse parameters.

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.add_blank_event
documentation:
Adds a blank event to the pulse sequence.

Args:
    event_name (str): The name of the event
    duration (float): The duration of the event with a unit suffix (n, u, m)

Returns:
    Event: The created event

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.add_pulse_event
documentation:
Adds a pulse event to the pulse sequence.

Args:
    event_name (str): The name of the event
    duration (float): The duration of the event with a unit suffix (n, u, m)
    amplitude (float): The amplitude of the transmit pulse in percent (min 0, max 100)
    phase (float): The phase of the transmit pulse
    shape (Function): The shape of the transmit pulse

Returns:
    Event: The created event

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.add_readout_event
documentation:
Adds a readout event to the pulse sequence.

Args:
    event_name (str): The name of the event
    duration (float): The duration of the event with a unit suffix (n, u, m)

Returns:
    Event: The created event

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_tx_amplitude
documentation:
Sets the relative amplitude of the transmit pulse in percent (larger 0 - max 100).

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the amplitude for or the name of the event
    amplitude (float): The amplitude of the transmit pulse in percent
TX Specific functions

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_tx_phase
documentation:
Sets the phase of the transmitter.

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the phase for or the name of the event
    phase (float): The phase of the transmitter

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_tx_shape
documentation:
Sets the shape of the transmit pulse.

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the shape for or the name of the event
    shape (Function): The shape of the transmit pulse

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_tx_n_phase_cycles
documentation:
Sets the number of phase cycles for the transmit pulse.

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the number of phase cycles for or the name of the event
    n_phase_cycles (int): The number of phase cycles

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_tx_phase_cycle_group
documentation:
Sets the phase cycle group for the transmit pulse.

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the phase cycle group for or the name of the event
    phase_cycle_group (int): The phase cycle group

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_rx
documentation:
Sets the receiver on or off.

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the receiver state for or the name of the event
    rx (bool): The receiver state
RX Specific functions

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.set_rx_phase
documentation:
Sets the phase of the receiver.

Args:
    event (Event | str): The event to set the phase for or the name of the event
    phase (list): The phase of the receiver

methods.name: quackseq.pulsesequence.QuackSequence.get_n_phase_cycles
documentation:
Returns the number of phase cycles of the pulse sequence.

Returns:
    int: The number of phase cycles

This is the Pulse Sequence that is compatible with all types of spectrometers.

If you want to implement your own spectrometer specific pulse sequence, you can inherit from the PulseSequence class.

modules.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer
documentation: The base class for the spectrometer used in quackseq. This class is just a skeleton and should be inherited by all spectrometer implementations.

classes.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer.Spectrometer

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer.Spectrometer.run_sequence
documentation:
Starts the measurement.

This method should be called when the measurement is started.

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer.Spectrometer.set_frequency
documentation: Sets the frequency of the spectrometer.

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer.Spectrometer.set_averages
documentation: Sets the number of averages.

Base class for spectrometers.

This class should be inherited by all spectrometers.
The spectrometers then need to implement the methods of this class.

modules.name: quackseq.event
documentation: Event class for the pulse sequence. Every pulse sequence consists of events, that are executed subsequently and have different parameters.

classes.name: quackseq.event.Event

methods.name: quackseq.event.Event.__init__
documentation: Initializes the event.

methods.name: quackseq.event.Event.init_pulse_parameters
documentation: Initializes the pulse parameters of the event.

methods.name: quackseq.event.Event.load_event
documentation:
Loads an event from a dict.

The pulse paramter options are needed to load the parameters
and determine if the correct spectrometer is active.

Args:
    event (dict): The dict with the event data
    pulse_sequence (PulseSequence): The pulse sequence the event belongs to

Returns:
    Event: The loaded event

methods.name: quackseq.event.Event.duration
documentation: The duration of the event.

An event is a part of a pulse sequence. It has a name and a duration and different parameters that have to be set.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the event
    duration (float | str): The duration of the event, either as a float or a string with a unit suffix (n, u, m)
    pulse_sequence (PulseSequence): The pulse sequence the event belongs to

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the event
    duration (str): The duration of the event
    pulse_sequence (PulseSequence): The pulse sequence the event belongs to

modules.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_settings
documentation: Settings for the different spectrometers.

classes.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_settings.Setting

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_settings.Setting.__init__
documentation:
Create a new setting.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the setting.
    category (str): The category of the setting.
    description (str): A description of the setting.
    default: The default value of the setting.

A setting for the spectrometer is a value that is the same for all events in a pulse sequence.

E.g. the Transmit gain or the number of points in a spectrum.

Args:
    name (str) : The name of the setting as it is used in the code
    category (str) : The category of the setting
    description (str) : A description of the setting
    default : The default value of the setting

Attributes:
    name (str) : The name of the setting
    category (str) : The category of the setting
    description (str) : A description of the setting
    value : The value of the setting
    category (str) : The category of the setting

classes.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_settings.NumericalSetting

methods.name: quackseq.spectrometer.spectrometer_settings.NumericalSetting.description_limit_info

Updates the description with the limits of the setting if there are any.

Args:
    description (str): The description of the setting.
    min_value: The minimum value of the setting.
    max_value: The maximum value of the setting.

Returns:
    str: The description of the setting with the limits.

A setting that is a numerical value.

It can additionally have a minimum and maximum value.

modules.name: quackseq.pulseparameters
documentation:
Contains the classes for the pulse parameters of the spectrometer. It includes the functions and the options for the pulse parameters.

Todo:
    * This shouldn't be in the spectrometer module. It should be in it"s own pulse sequence module.

classes.name: quackseq.pulseparameters.PulseParameter

methods.name: quackseq.pulseparameters.PulseParameter.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the pulse parameter.

Arguments:
    name (str) : The name of the pulse parameter

methods.name: quackseq.pulseparameters.PulseParameter.add_option
documentation:
Adds an option to the pulse parameter.

Args:
    option (Option) : The option to add

methods.name: quackseq.pulseparameters.PulseParameter.get_options
documentation:
Gets the options of the pulse parameter.

Returns:
    list : The options of the pulse parameter

methods.name: quackseq.pulseparameters.PulseParameter.get_option_by_name
documentation:
Gets an option by its name.

Args:
    name (str) : The name of the option

Returns:
    Option : The option with the specified name

Raises:
    ValueError : If no option with the specified name is found

methods.name: quackseq.pulseparameters.PulseParameter.update_option
documentation:
Generic update option method for pulse parameters.

This can be implemented by subclasses to update the options of the pulse parameter whenever the parameter is called (e.g. in the GUI).

Args:
    sequence (QuackSequence): The sequence to update the options from.

A pulse parameter is a value that can be different for each event in a pulse sequence.

E.g. the transmit pulse power or the phase of the transmit pulse.

Args:
    name (str) : The name of the pulse parameter

Attributes:
    name (str) : The name of the pulse parameter
    options (OrderedDict) : The options of the pulse parameter

documentation:
Gets the phase of the TX Pulse.

Returns:
    np.array : The phases of the TX Pulse

Basic TX Pulse Parameter. It includes options for the relative amplitude, the phase and the pulse shape.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the pulse parameter.

Args:

Basic PulseParameter for the RX Readout. It includes an option for the RX Readout state.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the pulse parameter.

Attributes:
    RX (str): The RX Readout state.

It adds an option for the Gate state.

Basic PulseParameter for the Gate. It includes an option for the Gate state.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the pulse parameter.

Attributes:
    GATE_STATE (str): The Gate state.

modules.name: quackseq.sequences.COMPFID
documentation:
Composite FID example.

This example demonstrates how to simulate a composite FID signal using the quackseq simulator.

See the paper:
Sauer, K.L., Klug, C.A., Miller, J.B. et al. Using quaternions to design composite pulses for spin-1 NQR. Appl. Magn. Reson. 25, 485–500 (2004). https://doi.org/10.1007/BF03166543

This also works for Samples with spin > 1.

A parameter of a function.

This can be for example the standard deviation of a Gaussian function.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the parameter.
    symbol (str): The symbol of the parameter.
    value (float): The value of the parameter.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the parameter.
    symbol (str): The symbol of the parameter.
    value (float): The value of the parameter.
    default (float): The default value of the parameter.

A function that can be used as a pulse parameter.

This class is the base class for all functions that can be used as pulse parameters. Functions can be used for pulse shapes, for example.

Args:
    expr (str | sympy.Expr): The expression of the function.

Attributes:
    name (str): The name of the function.
    parameters (list): The parameters of the function.
    expr (sympy.Expr): The sympy expression of the function.
    resolution (float): The resolution of the function in seconds.
    start_x (float): The x value where the evalution of the function starts.
    end_x (float): The x value where the evalution of the function ends.

classes.name: quackseq.functions.SincFunction

methods.name: quackseq.functions.SincFunction.__init__
documentation: Initializes the SincFunction.

The sinc function.

The sinc function is defined as sin(x * l) / (x * l).
The parameter is the scale factor l.

classes.name: quackseq.functions.GaussianFunction

methods.name: quackseq.functions.GaussianFunction.__init__
documentation: Initializes the GaussianFunction.

The Gaussian function.

The Gaussian function is defined as exp(-0.5 * ((x - mu) / sigma)**2).
The parameters are the mean and the standard deviation.

modules.name: quackseq.sequences.SEPC
documentation: A spin echo sequence with phase cycling.

functions.name: quackseq.sequences.SEPC.create_SEPC
documentation:
Creates a simple spin echo sequence with phase cycling.

The sequence consists of a pi/2 pulse, a pi pulse, and a readout event.

The phase cycling is set to cycle through 0°, 90°, 180°, 270° for the first pulse and constant 180° for the second pulse.
The readout phase for the different phase cycles is set to 0°, 90°, 180°, 270°.

Returns:
    QuackSequence: The SEPC sequence

modules.name: quackseq.options
documentation: Options for the pulse parameters. Options can be of different types, for example boolean, numeric or function. Generally pulse parameters have different values for the different events in a pulse sequence.

Defines a column option for a table option.

Args:
    name (str): The name of the option.
    type (type): The type of the option.
    default_value: The default value of the option.

A table option has rows and columns and can be used to store a table of values.

The table option acts as a 'meta' option, which means that we can add different types of options to the table as columns.
Associated with every row we can add a number of different values.
The number  of rows can be adjusted at runtime.

documentation: This module defines the measurement data structure and the fit class for measurement data.

methods.name: quackseq.measurement.Measurement.target_frequency
documentation: Target frequency of the measurement.

methods.name: quackseq.measurement.Measurement.fits
documentation: Fits of the measurement.

This class defines how measurement data should look.

It includes pulse parameters necessary for further signal processing.
Every spectrometer should adhere to this data structure in order to be compatible with the rest of the nqrduck.

Args:
    name (str): Name of the measurement.
    tdx (np.array): Time axis for the x axis of the measurement data.
    tdy (np.array): Time axis for the y axis of the measurement data. This can be multi-dimensional. Axis 0 are the different data sets (e.g. phase cycles).
    target_frequency (float): Target frequency of the measurement.
    frequency_shift (float, optional): Frequency shift of the measurement. Defaults to 0.
    IF_frequency (float, optional): Intermediate frequency of the measurement. Defaults to 0.

Attributes:
    tdx (np.array): Time axis for the x axis of the measurement data. Axis 0 are the different data sets (e.g. phase cycles).
    tdy (np.array): Time axis for the y axis of the measurement data. Axis 0 are the different data sets (e.g. phase cycles).
    target_frequency (float): Target frequency of the measurement.
    frequency_shift (float): Frequency shift of the measurement.
    IF_frequency (float): Intermediate frequency of the measurement.
    fdx (np.array): Frequency axis for the x axis of the measurement data.
    fdy (np.array): Frequency axis for the y axis of the measurement data.
